notes: 0.12.0 SHIPPED 2026-07-21 (session 26-07-21-002, impl commit 9cfe9ef): casp upgrade -- refresh a cockpit's scaffolds without eating its state. SEQUENCING WAS INVERTED, ON PURPOSE: the queue had facts-layer first (decision 2026-07-20, 'a demonstrated incident beats ergonomics'). That held while upgrade was hypothetical; 0.11.0 ended it. CASP-SESSION-003's adoption path runs through a CHANGED session-log template (the `phase:` frontmatter), and before this session no existing cockpit could receive it -- init refuses on an existing casp/ and --force overwrites every shipped file including state.json/now.md/roadmap.md. The rule shipped in 0.11.0 was unadoptable by every repo already on CASP. facts-layer would also have stacked a second undeliverable scaffold (casp/facts.json) on top. Queue is now facts-layer -> demand-gated-tail; next_prompt unchanged. THE VERB: refreshes ONLY casp/README.md and casp/templates/**, a list DERIVED from what the package ships minus a three-entry data denylist (state.json, now.md, roadmap.md, matched on full path AND basename), so a future scaffold is delivered without touching upgrade.ts. Never writes now.md/roadmap.md, never templates over state.json; the single state write is additive -- stamp casp_version, round-tripped through the parsed object. Never deletes, never writes through a symlink, idempotent, --dry-run/-n, exit 1 with an init pointer when there is no casp/, never gates. THE STAMP IS NAMESPACED: casp_version, not version -- state.json has always accepted arbitrary extra keys, so a bare `version` would have silently overwritten a project's own product version in the one verb that promises no existing value changes. Optional and never gating: an unstamped cockpit still PASSes check (pinned by a test; a required key would have reddened every existing user repo). doctor gains a cockpit.version probe -- PASS when current, WARN when older / unstamped / stamped by a NEWER casp than installed; never FAIL, doctor still always exits 0. THE SCAFFOLDED-ON DATE IS DATA, NOT A TEMPLATE VALUE: casp/README.md's date is read back out and re-used, otherwise the file would differ every day and the verb would never be idempotent. DELIBERATE DEVIATION FROM A WRITTEN MUST-HAVE: the prompt asked upgrade to materialize new optional keys with their defaults; it does not, because every optional key today (sessions_dir, logs_dir, migrations_dir, migrations_applied, last_deep_audit) has its ABSENCE for a default -- writing one would assert a claim the operator never made, and would be a data change the same MUST-HAVE forbids. Reasoning recorded in the session log, not only in a code comment. 102 -> 118 tests. check --json schema stays v1, no new rule. A read-only reviewer sub-agent audited the slice before commit (GO-WITH-FIXES); all five fixes folded in: uncaught write errors (EISDIR/EACCES/ENOTDIR) no longer abort the run half-refreshed AND unstamped -- same class the 0.11.0 audit fixed in check, guarded on read but not on write; the key renamed to casp_version; symlinks reported and left alone (they can point outside casp/); a README refresh line that says it replaces local edits; and a tripwire test pinning README.md as the only shipped non-data scaffold carrying {{TODAY}}, because date preservation is coupled to its **Scaffolded** line and a future placeholder elsewhere would kill idempotency silently. The audit also surfaced a gap outside the slice, fixed here: nothing asserted that the verbs help.ts documents and the verbs cli.ts dispatches are the same set -- the hardcoded list in test/help.test.mjs had fallen five verbs behind without failing. Now checked structurally in both directions. NOT PUBLISHED TO NPM -- 0.11.0 was not published either, so two minors are stacked and should go out together; publishing stays a separate CEO-gated act. next_prompt remains docs/plan/sessions/PHASE-FACTS-LAYER.md. Run node dist/cli.js check before push.
